```python
# Initialize the known quantities
total_standards = 0  # To be filled with the total number of standards from 1959
superseded_standards = 0  # To be filled with the number of superseded standards

# Calculate the percentage of superseded standards
if total_standards > 0:
    percentage_superseded = (superseded_standards / total_standards) * 100
else:
    percentage_superseded = 0

print(f"Percentage of superseded standards: {round(percentage_superseded)}%")
```

United States Standards for Grades: Processed VegetablesHomeMarket News Rules & RegulationsGrades & StandardsServicesResourcesCommodity ProcurementDocket Number: AMS–FV–14–0088
Effective Date: May 20, 2019
SummaryThe Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) of the Department of Agriculture (USDA) is revising the U.S. Standards for Grades of Canned Lima Beans, U.S. Standards for Grades of Canned Mushrooms, U.S. Standards for Grades of Pickles, and U.S. Standards for Grades of Green Olives. AMS is replacing the term “midget” with “petite” in the canned lima bean, canned mushroom, and pickle standards, and removing “midget” completely from the green olive standards as there is an alternative term. AMS is also replacing the two-term grading system (dual nomenclature) with a single term to describe each quality level in the canned lima bean, canned mushroom, and green olive standards. Editorial changes are also being made to the grade standards that conform to recent changes made in other grade standards.

The dried (or evaporated) apple segments may be cut further into smaller segments in preparation for dehydration whereby practically all of the moisture is removed to produce a very dry texture and are prepared to assure a clean, sound, wholesome product.
